name: attention-v2-benchmarks-20260620
tweets:
We froze TRADE benchmarks on H100 + H200.

Measured joules. Frozen JSON. Public ./run_bench.sh in 60s.

Headline: 16.8× less energy than Flash on compressible 16k RAG KV.

https://github.com/RegularJoe-CEO/attention-v2-benchmarks

@DrJimFan @pytorch @vllm_project @tri_dao

Kernel morph + CGA @ rag_tokenized seq=16,384 (H200):

• TRADE morph: 0.08 J, 0.65 ms
• Flash attn: 1.38 J, 6.41 ms
→ 16.8× joules, 9.8× wall time

Full layer morph-auto vs off: 2.06× joules (372 J vs 765 J).

Compressible KV only — see RESULTS_2026.md §11.

Sprint A vs PyTorch SDPA-math (attn-only, H200, seq=16k):

• rag_chunks: 438× joules
• clustered: 329×
• stable_prefix: 359×

0.08–0.11 J vs 36–38 J. Checksum-locked in repo.

Conservative baseline — geodesic full layer (H100, general KV):

• 3.2× lower joules/token vs PyTorch SDPA
• 6.8 ms vs vLLM ~8.2 ms
• O(N) @ 131k: 341× memory, 2048× energy vs naive N²
• AUDIT: max_diff 0.00e0 CPU↔CUDA

Flash wins raw fp16 attn. We win joules + determinism.

Verify yourself: https://github.com/RegularJoe-CEO/attention-v2-benchmarks — run ./run_bench.sh (60s, no engine needed).

Engine is proprietary — repo is results + verification.

luxiedge.com · DM @RegularJoe_Ceo
optional_reply:
Same stack, different lever: MumbleLang MBL/1.0 cuts effective attention seq 1.7–3.3× on structured prompts before geodesic pass.

Public verifier: ./run_bench.sh --mumble
https://github.com/RegularJoe-CEO/MumbleLang
